action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20 achieved mae_60min=3.6669 with best2.pt and no TTA, which is 0.0002 worse than the best score of 3.6667 (iter 19, best2.pt + TTA). We have ~6.5 minutes left and 10 iterations. Analysis: Before spending the remaining time on random TTA tweaks, I want to quickly quantify whether a simple, data-driven post-processing trick—blending model predictions with a naive last-value baseline per horizon—can improve the 60-minute MAE on the validation set. If it yields a clear gain, I can incorporate it into predict.py. This is fast to compute (~5-10s) and low-risk. Decision: Write and run compute_blend.py to grid-search an optimal per-step blending weight alpha between the best2.pt predictions and the naive repeated-last-value baseline on the validation set. No eval submission this iteration; just gather data for the next step.
